title: myrsine novocaledonica subsp. kaalaensis
intro: Myrsine novocaledonica subsp. kaalaensis, also known as the Kaala myrsine, is a rare and endemic plant species native to French Polynesia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Studies have shown that Kaala myrsine may have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, and is also used in traditional Polynesian remedies to treat a range of ailments.
